articles.title: Emerging-Market Stocks Mixed as Investors Weigh Iran Tensions
articles.summary: Emerging-market stocks and currencies were mixed as investors weighed rising tensions between the US and Iran, as well as a technology-led selloff that rattled global markets last week.

articles.title: Palm Climbs to Near One-Month High as Iran Conflict Boosts Crude
articles.summary: Palm oil rose to the highest in nearly a month, tracking renewed strength in crude and other vegetable oil prices as the US and Iran exchanged escalating military strikes.

articles.title: RBI Intervenes to Support Rupee as Currency Nears Record Low
articles.summary: India’s central bank stepped into the foreign exchange markets to support the rupee after the currency weakened toward a record low as a renewed surge in crude prices weighed on sentiment.

articles.title: Key China Copper Gauge Rallies to $100 After Tax Crackdown
articles.summary: A closely-watched gauge of China’s copper market has risen to the highest in more than a year, as a tax shake-up in the metal’s biggest market spurs a shortage of scrap and boosts demand for imports.

articles.title: NZD: Hedge Funds Amass Biggest New Zealand Dollar Net Short Since 2006
articles.summary: Leveraged funds have taken their short positions on the New Zealand dollar to a record high, betting that a recent rebound in global oil prices may exacerbate domestic economic pressures.
